title: cymopterus purpureus
intro: Cymopterus purpureus, also known as purple spring-parsley, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and native to the western United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high levels of antioxidants and vitamins, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Cymopterus purpureus can be prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, and it is also used as a culinary herb in some recipes.
